person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Robert Honywood, Esq. was born in Postling, Kent, England, burial in April 1576, St Mary's, Lenham, Kent. He was the son of John Honywood (born 1466) and Alice Barnes. Robert married Mary, daughter of Robert Atwater of Lenham and Royton, Kent. Mary was the youngest daughter of Robert Atwater, she brought the estates of Lenham, the manor of Pett's and Newcourt to her marriage.

«b»Children:«/b»
1.) Robert Honywood, married 1st Dorathy Crooke, 2nd Elizabeth Browne

2.) Anthony Honywood, married Ann Gibson, widow

3.) Arther Honywood married Eliza Spencer

4.) Isaak Honywood, slain at the Battle of Newport

5.) Catherine Honywood, married 1st William Fleet, 2nd William Henmarsh

6.) Priscillia Honywood, married Sir Thomas Engham

7.) Mary Honywood, married George Morton

8.) Anne Honywood, married Sir Charles Hales, Knt.

9.) Grace Honywood, married Michael Heneage

10.) Elizabeth Honywood, married George Woodward

11.) Susan Honywood, married Kerchen

12.) Bennet Honywood, married Henry Croke

13.) Dorothy Honywood, married William Croke

«b»Death«/b»
Robert Honywood's eldest son and heir Robert wrote: "My father departed this lief on Easter day in ye afternoone, ye 22 of April 1576 at Pet in Charing." He was buried at Lenham, where his widow Mary was laid after him in 1620, although a memorial to her was made at Markshall, Essex.

sourceList.title: The Lovelace-Loveless and allied families : Robertson, Florance Alice Loveless Keeney, 1884

zh.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Honywood Esq是John Honywood和Alice Barnes的兒子，於1525年9月18日出生在英國肯特波斯特林。他於1543年2月與Mary Atwater在英國肯特肯特結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和16個女兒的父母。他去世於1576年4月22日在英國肯特加林享年50歲，埋葬在英國肯特倫哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Honywood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Honeywood in Postling (Kent), which was named in Old English with hunig ‘honey’ + wudu ‘wood’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Honywood Esq was born on 18 September 1525, in Postling, Kent, England as the son of John Honywood and Alice Barnes. He married Mary Atwater in February 1543, in Kent, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 16 daughters. He died on 22 April 1576, in Charing, Kent, England, at the age of 50, and was buried in Lenham, Kent, England.

Il a épousé Mary Atwater en février 1543, à Kent, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 16 filles. Il est décédé le 22 avril 1576, à Charing, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Lenham, Kent, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Honywood Esq(은)는 1525년 9월 18일 영국 켄트 포스틀링에서 John Honywood(와)과 Alice Barnes의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1543년 2월 영국 켄트 켄트에서 Mary Atwater(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1576년 4월 22일 영국 켄트 처잉에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 렌함에 묻혔다.

pt.St Mary's Church, Nonington, Kent, Engla: St Mary's Church, Nonington, Kent, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Honywood Esq nasceu em 18 de setembro de 1525, em Postling, Kent, Inglaterra filho de John Honywood e Alice Barnes. Ele casou-se com Mary Atwater em fevereiro de 1543, em Kent, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 16 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de abril de 1576, em Charing, Kent, Inglaterra, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Lenham, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.St Mary's Church, Nonington, Kent, Engla: ст мэри'с чурч, нонингтон, кэнт, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Honywood Esq родился 18 сентября 1525 г. в местности постлинг, кэнт, Англия. Его родителей звали John Honywood и Alice Barnes. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Atwater февраль 1543 г. в местности кэнт, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 16 дочерей. Он умер 22 апреля 1576 г. в местности чаринг, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности ленам, кэнт, Англия.
